title: avenella stricta subsp. mairei
intro: Avenella stricta subsp. mairei, also known as the Moorland hair grass or Maire's hair grass, is a subspecies of the Avenella stricta plant, a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. The health benefits of Avenella stricta subsp. mairei include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and promoting a good night's sleep. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to alleviate respiratory distress.
